title: Lemma 1 (lem:linear): diagonal pairings $\omega(v_i,u_i)\neq0$ not validly proved; rank-3 case ignored

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the proof of `lem:linear` the step "if some $\omega(v_i,u_i)=0$, then $\omega$ would vanish identically on a 3-dimensional subspace of $\mathbb R^4$, which is impossible" is **logically invalid**. From the four Lagrangian-face relations (cyclic order $v_1,v_2,u_1,u_2$: $\omega(v_1,v_2)=\omega(v_2,u_1)=\omega(u_1,u_2)=\omega(u_2,v_1)=0$) together with $\omega(v_1,u_1)=0$, one only gets that $v_1$ is $\omega$-orthogonal to $\mathrm{span}(v_2,u_1,u_2)$, i.e. $v_1$ lies in the *radical* of the 4-vector configuration. That is NOT the same as '$\omega$ vanishes on a 3-dim subspace' (which would be impossible but is a different claim).

The correct argument needs rank 4: if the four edge tangents span $\mathbb R^4$, a nonzero radical vector contradicts nondegeneracy. But the hypothesis is only '$\Sigma$ not contained in a single plane', i.e. rank $\ge 3$. Numerical least-squares search shows that imposing the four face relations plus $\omega(v_1,u_1)=0$ forces the configuration to **rank exactly 3** and simultaneously $\omega(v_2,u_2)=0$ — precisely the boundary case the proof must exclude. The rank-3 case is never handled.

Fix: (1) repair the linear-algebra step as above; (2) separately rule out rank 3 using the embedded-topological-manifold + exactly-4-faces hypothesis (a rank-3, hence coplanar-degenerate, configuration of four Lagrangian quadrants is not an embedded surface link).
